This is really an incredible museum. Outstanding exhibits about the European Jewish experience during WWII, as well as a moving exhibit on Anne Frank's diary. The museum moves on from WWII to other topics, including a California schools case asking to open "white" schools to Latin students. They won and it was a precursor to Brown v. Board of Education. There's also an interactive social lab with lots of interactive exhibits (and a lot of student groups- hurray!). The exhibits in this section urge action on the part of participants in many current social issues.
